statement.p: Find the y-intercept and any x-intercept(s) of the quadratic curve.

solution.p: To find a parabolas y-intercept, we substitute x=0 into {y}={x^{2}+5x-8}, and we have y=-8.
To find a parabolas x-intercept, we substitute y=0 into {y}={x^{2}+5x-8}, and then solve for x:
The polynomial cannot be factored, so we use the Quadratic Formula to solve this equation.

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned}[t] x \amp = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^{2}-4ac}}{2a} \\ x \amp = \frac{-(5) \pm \sqrt{(5)^{2}-4(1)(-8)}}{2(1)} \\ x \amp = \frac{-5 \pm \sqrt{57}}{2} \\ x \amp \approx 1.27491721763537 \text{ or } x \approx -6.27491721763537 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: The parabola has two x-intercepts: (1.27491721763537,0) and (-6.27491721763537,0).
